operation: Headstock-driven deep-bore drilling, then turn-between-centers off bore
tooling: MT2 + Jacobs chuck + tailstock vise; lathe between centers
notes: Bore first; round-turn after. Leave 0.5 in of waste at each end for chucking.
Square within 0.005 in. Foot defines the acoustic open end.

operation: Bandsaw rough-cut, chisel inner bevel, file flat
tooling: 1/4 in bandsaw blade, 1/2 in bench chisel, mill-bastard file
notes: Cut shallow first; deepen and re-tune. Inner bevel angle is set against printed 32 deg gauge.
Hole indexed off bore axis. File-open during tuning to ~0.30-0.35 in final.
Same as above; expect more file-open since shorter hole-to-foot is more sensitive.
Hole 3 sees the most filing on a NAF analog; budget time.
Wall thickness checkpoint — walls thinnest here on conical-taper builds; not an issue on this straight bore.
Drilled on the BACK face. Rotate jig 180 deg from front-hole axis.

operation: Mill mortise; press-fit with hide glue
tooling: 1/8 in straight router bit or chisel + mallet
notes: Skip for first prototype if no inlay decision made. Mortise depth = inlay thickness.
One-time build. Reusable across the family.
One-time build. V-cradle radius matches Ø 1.10 OD.
